Then he moved to Hollywood, worked as a cab driver and waiter. His father told him to stop the "craziness" and get a job, especially since he had a wife and daughter. His brother was murdered in 1983, which led him to write "Half-Deserted Streets" about his brother's killing. That play opened in 1988 Off-Broadway. A producer saw it and asked him to work on a screenplay. He eventually worked on "Crash" with Paul Haggis, which won several awards, making him an overnight success at 54.
